back_translation: Taking pride is necessary; though not beneficial, I will speak about visions and revelations from the Lord.
I know a person who is in Christ. Fourteen years ago — whether in the body or not in the body, I do not know, God knows — such a person was caught up to the third heaven.
And I know that such a person — whether in the body or outside the body, I do not know, God knows —
that he was caught up to the city of supreme bliss, and heard inexpressible words, which a person is not permitted to speak.
I will take pride concerning such a person, but I will not take pride concerning myself, except in my weaknesses.
For if I wanted to take pride, I would not be a fool, because I would speak the truth. But I restrain myself, so that no one would consider me to be greater than what he sees in me or hears from me.
And so that I would not exalt myself because of these surpassingly great revelations, a thorn came into my flesh, a messenger of Satan, to torment me, so that I would not exalt myself.
I pleaded to the Lord three times concerning this matter, that this thing would depart from me.

He said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, because power is made complete in weakness.' Therefore, I will take pride in my weaknesses with greater gladness, so that the power of Christ would come to dwell upon me.
Therefore, for the sake of Christ, I rejoice in weaknesses, in being insulted, in distresses, in being persecuted, and in pressings. For when I am weak, then I am strong.
I have become a fool; you forced me to do so. For in fact, you should have commended me, because I am not inferior to those 'top-tier apostles' even in the slightest, although I am nothing.
The signs of an apostle were demonstrated among you with all endurance, namely signs, miracles, and powers.
For in what matter were you inferior to the other churches, except that I myself was not a burden to you? Forgive me for this offense.

Behold, this is the third time I am ready to come to you, and I will not be a burden, because I do not seek your possessions, but seek you yourselves. For children are not required to store up wealth for their parents, but parents store up for their children.
I will spend and be utterly spent with greatest gladness for the lives of you. If I love you more, will I be loved less?
Let it be so; I was not a burden to you — but because I am crafty, I caught you with trickery.
Have I taken advantage of you through anyone whom I sent to you?
I asked Titus, and sent the brother along too. Did Titus take advantage of you? Did we not walk in the same spirit? In the same footsteps, did we not?
Have you been thinking all along that we are making a defense before you? We speak before the face of God in Christ. As for all these things, beloved ones, it is for the building up of you.

For I fear that, when I come, I may not find you in the way I want, and you may not find me in the way you want — fearing that there may be quarreling, jealousy, fits of anger, rivalry-seeking, slander, whispered slander, arrogance, and disorder.

I fear that when I come again, my God will humble me before you, and I will mourn over many people who have sinned previously, and have not repented from the impurity, sexual immorality, and debauchery which they did.
